initiative.name: All-Type RAG Upgrade
initiative.goal: Upgrade the current text-first RAG system into a structured, multimodal-capable document system that can ingest text, scanned PDFs, images, tables, and spreadsheets with reliable retrieval and source grounding.
tracking_notes: Use this file as the execution tracker for the all-type RAG push.
Do not mark Tier 3 complete until parser-layer, OCR, spreadsheet ingestion, and multimodal retrieval metadata are proven on real documents.
Qdrant, retrieval eval, and retrieval cleanup are groundwork, not the finish line.

tasks.title: Retrieval baseline and evaluation harness
tasks.summary: Benchmark retrieval quality before parser and multimodal changes.
tasks.why: Tier 3 work must be measured against a stable retrieval baseline.
deliverables: Repeatable retrieval benchmark
Sample dataset and metrics
Profile comparison for vector, hybrid, rerank, and query-transform
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark runs from repo scripts
Metrics include hit@k, recall@k, MRR, and nDCG
Results are documented in repo docs

tasks.title: Vector backend upgrade to Qdrant
tasks.summary: Replace FAISS-only runtime dependency with a vector-store seam and active Qdrant backend.
tasks.why: All-type RAG will need stronger persistence and metadata handling than FAISS-only local files.
deliverables: Vector-store abstraction
Qdrant implementation
Config-driven backend selection
acceptance_criteria: App starts with vector_store.backend=qdrant
Existing indexed docs can be restored into the new backend
Retrieval benchmark runs on Qdrant

tasks.title: Parser-layer abstraction before chunking
tasks.summary: Introduce a parser contract that outputs structured content blocks instead of immediately collapsing everything into plain text.
tasks.why: This is the gate for OCR, tables, images, Excel, and layout-aware chunking.
deliverables: Parser interface and structured block schema
Document element model for text, table, image, sheet, heading, and page blocks
Refactor upload/index flow to parse first, chunk second
acceptance_criteria: Chunking no longer depends on raw file-type branching only
Parsed blocks preserve source metadata like page number, section, and content type
Current PDF, DOCX, MD, and TXT flows still work through the new parser seam
progress_notes: Structured parser and element models are now live in chunker.py.
Document records and source traces now expose parser, content-type, and structural-role metadata.
TXT, Markdown, DOCX, and PDF flows have been validated through the new parser seam.
The parser seam is now the stable base for OCR, spreadsheets, and multimodal block handling.

tasks.title: Structured PDF parser for layout-aware extraction
tasks.summary: Upgrade PDF ingestion from plain text extraction to layout-aware block extraction.
tasks.why: Enterprise PDFs are rarely just linear text; headings, captions, page structure, and embedded elements matter.
deliverables: Improved PDF parser path
Structured page and block metadata
Graceful fallback when rich parsing is unavailable
acceptance_criteria: PDF parsing preserves page boundaries
Headings and structural blocks are represented explicitly
Chunks can reference the original PDF page or section
progress_notes: PdfDocumentParser now uses PyPDF2 visitor callbacks to collect positioned text fragments and page rectangles.
PDF text is grouped into lines and layout-aware blocks instead of collapsing directly into plain text.
PDF blocks now preserve page-level geometry and block metadata and fall back to plain-text extraction if visitor-based parsing fails.
A generated PDF smoke test verified explicit heading/body block extraction and page-aware chunk metadata.

tasks.title: OCR support for scanned PDFs and image-only documents
tasks.summary: Add OCR-capable parsing for image-only PDFs and standalone image documents.
tasks.why: Without OCR, scanned enterprise documents are effectively invisible to the system.
deliverables: OCR pipeline for scanned PDFs
Image document ingestion path
OCR confidence and provenance metadata
acceptance_criteria: Image-only PDF pages produce indexed text blocks
OCR output retains page or image references
Failed OCR is visible through processing status and errors
progress_notes: Added a Windows built-in OCR path through powershell.exe and Windows.Media.Ocr, without adding a new Python dependency.
Standalone image files now parse through an OCR-backed image parser.
Text-layer-empty PDF pages now fall back to OCR on extracted embedded page images.
Validation passed on a generated OCR image and a scanned-style PDF that contained only an embedded JPEG.

tasks.title: Spreadsheet ingestion for XLSX and CSV-like structured files
tasks.summary: Add spreadsheet parsing with sheet, column, and row-aware metadata.
tasks.why: Excel support is part of the all-type RAG promise and currently missing.
deliverables: XLSX parser path
Sheet-level and table-level content blocks
Chunk metadata for workbook, sheet, and table ranges
acceptance_criteria: Users can upload XLSX documents successfully
Indexed content can cite workbook and sheet context
Retrieval can return spreadsheet-derived chunks with clear provenance
progress_notes: Added a spreadsheet parser with no new dependency by reading XLSX as ZIP/XML directly.
Added delimited table parsing for CSV and TSV files with the standard library.
Spreadsheet blocks now preserve workbook, sheet, row-range, and column-count metadata.
Validation passed on generated XLSX and CSV samples.

tasks.title: Image, figure, and table-aware content blocks
tasks.summary: Represent images, figures, and tables as first-class parsed elements instead of letting them disappear into text extraction.
tasks.why: This is the bridge between parser upgrades and later multimodal retrieval.
deliverables: Content-type metadata for text, table, image, and figure blocks
Separate chunking policy for structural versus narrative content
Source serialization that exposes block type
acceptance_criteria: Retrieved sources show content type metadata
Table and image-derived blocks are distinguishable from plain text blocks
Chunking rules can vary by block type
progress_notes: Chunking is now modality-aware instead of treating all parsed elements like generic prose.
Headings are kept atomic, table-derived blocks preserve row boundaries, and OCR or image-derived blocks preserve line grouping.
Chunk metadata now records chunk_strategy alongside parser, content type, and structural role.
Validation passed on heading, spreadsheet-table, and OCR-backed image samples.

tasks.title: Metadata-rich chunk schema and retrieval filters
tasks.summary: Upgrade chunk metadata and retrieval filtering so new content types remain explorable and debuggable.
tasks.why: All-type ingestion becomes unmanageable if chunk metadata cannot express modality, provenance, and structure.
deliverables: Chunk metadata keys for modality and structure
Retrieval-side support for richer metadata
Source serialization updates for UI and APIs
acceptance_criteria: Metadata filter fields include structured document attributes
Evaluation can target specific content types later
Source traces show page, sheet, or block identity where available
progress_notes: Retriever metadata filters now support operator-style conditions such as eq, in, gte, lte, and between.
The active Qdrant backend now translates supported metadata filters into payload filters during vector search.
Config now explicitly allows filtering by parser, content type, structure, page, sheet, row-range, chunk strategy, and OCR/layout metadata.
Validation passed on a temporary Qdrant corpus using structured filters for table and OCR-image retrieval.

tasks.title: Multimodal evaluation dataset and regression suite
tasks.summary: Expand the evaluation harness to cover scanned PDFs, image-heavy documents, and spreadsheets.
tasks.why: Tier 3 claims are meaningless without representative evaluation data.
deliverables: Evaluation corpus with non-text-heavy documents
Gold queries for tables, images, OCR, and spreadsheets
Regression path for parser-level changes
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark includes multimodal or structured document cases
Tier 3 work can be measured against the baseline harness
Parser regressions are visible in evaluation output
progress_notes: Added generated multimodal fixtures for OCR image, scanned PDF, XLSX, and CSV regression cases.
Added a multimodal dataset with query-level metadata filters so retrieval can target specific modalities and structures.
Added a multimodal evaluation suite that generates fixtures, runs parser smoke checks, and then executes the retrieval benchmark.
Validation passed on the multimodal dataset for vector_only and hybrid retrieval profiles.

tasks.title: Upload and workspace UX for all-type documents
tasks.summary: Expose parse status, content types, and richer library states in the frontend.
tasks.why: Users need to know whether a document was text-parsed, OCR-parsed, spreadsheet-parsed, or partially failed.
deliverables: Library indicators for parse mode and content type
Document status details for OCR and parser failures
Source drawer support for richer provenance
acceptance_criteria: Users can see what kind of document processing happened
Failure states are visible without opening logs
Source drawer reflects richer parser metadata
progress_notes: The library drawer now shows parse-mode badges derived from parser and content-type summaries.
Document failure states are surfaced inline instead of being buried in generic metadata text.
Source traces now render richer provenance badges for parser, modality, chunk strategy, page, sheet, row range, and OCR source.
Validation passed through a frontend production build.

tasks.title: PostgreSQL-ready chat history abstraction
tasks.summary: Add a persistence seam for chat and project history so SQLite can be replaced cleanly later.
tasks.why: This is part of the roadmap and should happen before SaaS auth and tenanting.
deliverables: History storage contract
SQLite adapter kept as current implementation
Postgres-ready interface
acceptance_criteria: rag_service no longer depends directly on SQLite-specific methods
Current project/session workflows still work
Postgres implementation can be added without another large service refactor
progress_notes: Introduced a HistoryStore protocol and factory in chat_history_db.py.
RAGApplication now depends on an injected history-store contract instead of constructing the SQLite class directly.
The current SQLite implementation remains the active adapter with no behavior change for project, session, or message flows.
Validation passed through Python compile checks and a temp-store smoke test.

tasks.title: Graph retrieval decision gate
tasks.summary: Decide whether graph retrieval is justified after parser and multimodal evaluation results exist.
tasks.why: Graph retrieval is expensive complexity and should not be started without evidence.
deliverables: Decision memo based on evaluation results
Problem statement for graph-specific retrieval gaps
Prototype scope only if justified
acceptance_criteria: Evaluation identifies real unanswered cross-document problems
Graph retrieval is either approved with clear scope or explicitly deferred
progress_notes: The baseline retrieval harness, multimodal ingestion work, structured filters, and multimodal regression suite are now all in place.
Current evaluation evidence does not show a graph-shaped failure mode; the active bottlenecks are still parser coverage and larger enterprise corpora.
Graph retrieval is explicitly deferred until future evaluation proves relationship-heavy cases that standard retrieval, reranking, and metadata filtering cannot solve.
